title: Book Trilogy Showcase
public_claim: Book Trilogy Showcase is the reader-facing exhibit for a governed three-book manuscript system.
distinctive_mechanism: It presents the trilogy through public book order, canonical IDs, seventy-two chapter profiles, ReaderEdition targets, FinalProof argument backbone, ConstructionKits, PassageTargets, Democratic Dialectic voice material, book-specific registers, chapter shape, bridge logic, dependency logic, terminology controls, visual identity, and rewrite-state limits.

component_demo_cases.claim_to_demonstrate: keeps the three-book system stable while public titles and presentation language are improved
component_demo_cases.input_fixture: 72 canonical chapter IDs, public book order, book-title logic, and chapter-title state
component_demo_cases.operation_under_test: keeps the three-book system stable while public titles and presentation language are improved
component_demo_cases.expected_output: chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping
component_demo_cases.evidence_locator: Expected proof artifact: chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping
runbook: build or run a demonstration that produces the specified output
Use this input fixture: 72 canonical chapter IDs, public book order, book-title logic, and chapter-title state
Verify that the component produces: chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping
Preserve the evidence at or beside: Expected proof artifact: chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping
acceptance_criteria: The input, operation, and expected output are all visible.
The evidence locator is present or the missing evidence is explicitly named.
The result supports the component claim without implying broader completion.
component_demo_cases.claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
component_demo_cases.next_build_task: Apply Canonical Chapter Identity to one chapter dossier; confirm title, visual, source state, rewrite target, and handoff decision.
